title: RaaS cleaning robots: subscription pricing and fit
metaDescription: RaaS cleaning robots explained: monthly subscription ranges, what is included, when cleaning-as-a-service beats buying, and how to evaluate contracts.
intro: RaaS cleaning — robotics-as-a-service for scrubbers and vacuums — bundles hardware, software, and often maintenance into a monthly fee. It is popular for first deployments and sites with variable cleaning demand.

sections.heading: What RaaS cleaning includes
bullets: Robot hardware for the contract term
Maintenance and often a swap unit

sections.heading: Typical monthly ranges
paragraphs: Published ranges are illustrative — vendors quote per site. Use them to budget before sales calls, not as final pricing.
bullets: Shorter contracts often carry higher monthly fees

sections.heading: When RaaS cleaning makes sense
bullets: First autonomous cleaning pilot on one building

sections.heading: Contract questions to ask
bullets: Response time and spare robot policy
Buyout or purchase option at end of term
Minimum term and early termination fees

faqs.question: What does RaaS cleaning mean?
faqs.answer: Robotics-as-a-service for cleaning: you pay a monthly subscription for autonomous scrubbers or vacuums, usually with maintenance and software included, instead of buying hardware outright.

faqs.question: Is RaaS cleaning worth it?
faqs.answer: It is strong for pilots and variable demand. High-utilization sites over many years often save money buying — if they can absorb maintenance risk.
